g.text: ── CPU Exception ── INT 14 (Page Fault) ── Privilege Ring 3 → Ring 0 ──

div.p: ⚠️ Critical: Page Table Ordering Signal check runs BEFORE set_pdir_base() so kernel can still access user memory via identity-mapped physical addresses
⚠️ Critical: Queue Guard tqueue_remove() only called if tcb_get_state == TSTATE_READY Otherwise destroys the entire ready queue (head/tail → NUM_IDS)

pre.code: int *ptr = NULL; volatile int val = *ptr; // 💥 FAULT

div.p: CPU pushes to kernel stack: SS → ESP → EFLAGS → CS → EIP → errno Assembly: pushal saves all registers Result: Trap Frame (tf_t) constructed
Fault VA = 0x00000000 (NULL) Sets bit 11 in pending_signals[7]

p.strong: No user handler → kernel handles it

pre.code: 1. tcb_set_state(pid, TSTATE_DEAD) 2. if (state == TSTATE_READY) tqueue_remove(NUM_IDS, pid) // Guard! 3. thread_exit() // Switch away, never return

div.p: Queue Guard: prevents destroying ready queue when removing RUNNING process

pre.code: 1. tqueue_dequeue(NUM_IDS) → next_pid = 2 (shell) 2. tcb_set_state(2, TSTATE_RUN) 3. set_curid(2) 4. kctx_switch(old_kctx, new_kctx) → CPU switches to shell

div.p: Does NOT re-enqueue dead process (unlike thread_yield which would!)

text.tspan: Shell prints: OS is still running!
